title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Graph Lens: Tag Target Movement
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git tag target movement increment in a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Tag Target Movement while producing a signed-off release evidence record.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. Competency probe: Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations. A defensible solution applies Tag Target Movement, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-off release evidence record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: git-tag-target-movement — Create, inspect, verify, delete, and cautiously replace tags while recognizing immutable-release expectations.
git status --porcelain=v1
git show --no-patch --format=fuller HEAD
git tag -a "v3.1.0" -m 'Verified release candidate' HEAD
git show --no-patch "v3.1.0^{commit}"
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
